text_chunks.grading_and_syllabus: 20% Midterm, 20% Final, 20% Quiz, 30% Individual Assignment, 10% Attendance
text_chunks.student_reviews: Highly positive sentiment (4.83/5). Very generous absolute grading and manageable exams. However, lectures are slow-paced, and the professor tends to read straight from textbook PPTs, requiring heavily independent study.

Exceptional lecture quality (4.75/5) but notorious for extreme exam difficulty. Explains complex theoretical concepts thoroughly. Highly generous grading grants A+ even to subpar outputs to balance the brutal exams.
Excellent delivery with high-quality materials (4.50/5). Workload is demanding with time-consuming coding assignments and TA interviews, but grading is incredibly smooth and generous.
Mixed/frustrated sentiment. Very high mathematical barrier assuming significant prior knowledge. Features high discrimination power on exams and strictly capped grades, making it highly competitive.
Generally well-liked for its light workload and engaging, friendly teaching style. However, notorious for an exceptionally strict electronic attendance policy that heavily impacts grades.

text_chunks.grading_and_syllabus: 30% Midterm, 30% Final, 18% Individual Assignment, 10% Team Project, 5% Quiz, 5% Attendance, 2% Q/A
text_chunks.student_reviews: Overwhelmingly negative sentiment (4.36/5) characterized by a frustrating flipped-classroom structure. Highly criticized for ambiguous quizzes, severe time constraints on exams, and punitive relative grading.

text_chunks.grading_and_syllabus: 30% Midterm, 30% Final, 10% Individual Assignment, 20% Team Assignment, 10% Attendance
text_chunks.student_reviews: Highly critical sentiment (2.12/5.00). Most students describe it as a low-value course with an old-fashioned mindset from the teaching staff. It covers a shallow, liberal-arts-style historical overview of AI rather than deep engineering concepts, making it easy to cram for exams via rote memorization.

text_chunks.grading_and_syllabus: 20% Midterm, 30% Final, 10% Quiz, 20% Individual Assignment, 20% Team Assignment
text_chunks.student_reviews: Highly critical historical reviews regarding unorganized course structures and material, though recent semesters show visible improvements in content depth. The professor takes an abstract, freestyled approach that frequently shifts between English and Korean. Operations can feel chaotic due to sudden cancellations and backloaded deadlines.

Fanatically positive reviews (5.00/5.00) with intense praise, with multiple students calling it the finest senior-level lecture at the university. Features impeccable instructional delivery paired with high-quality presentation decks. While the cumulative midterm exam is notoriously difficult and can cause panic, the overall grading is incredibly forgiving.

text_chunks.alternative_professor_reviews: Deducts marks despite unclear assignment requirements, passionate about teaching and receptive to feedback, very strict attendance policy, provides vague project instructions while expecting high-quality work.

text_chunks.grading_and_syllabus: 100% Team Project (Evaluated via Research Proposals, Progress Supervision, and Final Exhibition)
text_chunks.student_reviews: Highly polarized reviews regarding intense workload and project assignment clarity, but universally praised for ultimate grading generosity. The lecturing delivery faces criticism for an exceptionally slow pace and stuttering pronunciation. Programming specifications can be confusing, forcing complete reliance on independent troubleshooting and team collaboration.

responsive to questions, good teaching quality, high difficulty, professor good at explaining, test questions are mostly proof questions, taking courses like probability theory, linear algebra and calculus will help a lot, fair grading distribution

metadata.name: SW-AI BUSINESS APPLICATION DESIGN

text_chunks.student_reviews: no exams, only presentations for midterms and finals, professor is responsive and helpful, aim of class is to create your own startup product, certain level of coding experience especially in web development is required, generous with grades as long as you put in the effort
